name: Red Chile Risotto Clams
recipeIngredient: 7 Cups Clam Juice or Fish Stock
½ Cup White Onion; Finely Diced
4 Tablespoons Hot New Mexico Chile Powder
recipeInstructions:
Or you can use Medium Molido and cayenne mixed.
The Parmesan cheese should be finely ground or grated.
1. Scrub the clams and discard any dead ones (see Recipe 16).
2. In a wide bottomed stainless steel or enamel pan, combine the sliced garlic, parsley, 4 cups of water, white wine, and clams in a single layer.
3. Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat. Simmer until the clams open, no more than 4–5 minutes.
4. Turn off the heat and let sit for an additional 5–10 minutes. Remove the clams from the broth and extract their meat; discard any clam shells that remain closed (they may be bad). Allow the broth to cool, remove garlic and parsley if necessary, and strain out sand or other particles.
5. Mix the cooled broth with enough clam juice so that the total liquid volume is 11 cups.
6. In a large pan over medium heat, sauté the onion with 3 tablespoons butter until soft (about 15 minutes).
7. Add the rice and cook for 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Cook on medium heat, adding 2 cups water and 2 cups clam juice alternately, stirring continuously until absorbed (about 10 minutes). Then add another 4 cups of liquid—2 cups water and 2 cups clam juice—in half-cup increments, stirring and allowing each portion to absorb before adding the next.
8. At this point, the rice should be about half cooked. If preparing ahead, spread it on a flat sheet pan to prevent clumping.
9. Add minced garlic, tomatoes, and chile powder; continue cooking over medium heat for 10 minutes.
10. Gradually add another 4 cups of clam juice in quarter-cup increments, stirring and allowing each portion to absorb before adding the next (another 8–10 minutes).
11. Add the remaining clams and the last cup of clam juice in quarter-cup increments, letting the liquid absorb for 2–3 minutes.
12. Remove from heat, stir in any remaining butter and grated Parmesan cheese. Garnish with parsley chopped on top.
13. Serve hot.
